policy.text: Always handle boundary cases explicitly—especially empty inputs—according to the required return contract before applying operations that assume data exists.
Use failing tests to infer ambiguous output requirements—here, “unique values” must be returned in sorted order, not insertion order.

curve.lesson: Always handle boundary cases explicitly—especially empty inputs—according to the required return contract before applying operations that assume data exists.
Handle invalid or malformed inputs explicitly and return the specified sentinel value instead of allowing parsing exceptions to escape.
Use failing tests to infer ambiguous output requirements—here, “unique values” must be returned in sorted order, not insertion order.
Match the required output precision explicitly—when tests expect a percentage like 33.33, round the computed value to two decimal places.
